steps.title: Identify what the student's claim requires
steps.content:
The student claims that some modern irrigation methods have a lineage that long predates Lake Marisol's completion in 1908. Any supporting quotation must show that techniques in use today trace back to a time well before 1908.
  • Choice A — ‘"Sprinkler irrigation systems, which were developed in the late 20th century, are a convenient technique of irrigating that makes use of machinery in order to spray water in all directions. Many areas of the Southwestern United States are currently irrigated by this method."’: Describes a modern technique developed after 1908 — establishes no pre-1908 lineage. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘"The importance of the development of irrigation infrastructure in the American Southwest today cannot be overstated, since it is the most common means of conveying water for food production."’: Stresses present-day significance only, with no historical depth. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘"In the decades following the Second World War, irrigation of arid areas for agriculture increased to such a degree that it now accounts for roughly 70% of the world's water usage."’: References mid-20th-century growth — still after 1908, so no pre-1908 lineage. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘"The irrigation system developed by the Hohokam people in what is now central Arizona in the 7th century CE was simple but made use of hydraulic engineering design principles that are still utilized by today's engineers."’: Explicitly links a 7th-century system to principles engineers use now — directly proves the long pre-1908 lineage. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only the credited quotation provides evidence that current irrigation methods descend from techniques centuries older than 1908. D is correct.
